original: Pope Gregory II names Boniface a missionary bishop
Pope Innocent III closes the Fourth Council of the Lateran
Angelo Correr elected Pope Gregory XII
Amsterdam bans assembly of heretics
16,000 inhabitants of Venice die this month (Nov 1630) from the plague
Roman Catholics are banned from the English Parliament
Utrecht, Overijssel, Buren, Leerdam and IJsselstein go on Gregoria calendar
Beijing hit by an earthquake; about 100,000 die
States of Holland forbid Free Masonry
Dutch State of Zealand declare governorship hereditary for women
Britain signs agreement recognizing US independence
Spanish governor leaves the Philippines
Spain cedes her claims to Louisiana Territory to France
Impeachment trial of Supreme Court Justice Samuel Chase begins
Prince Willem Frederik returns to Netherlands
First ground is broken at Allenburg for the building of the original Welland Canal
First Welland Canal opens for a trial run, 5 years to the day from the groundbreaking
Mexico declares war on France
Rebellion of miners at Eureka Stockade at Ballarat in Victoria who swear allegiance to the Southern Cross Flag, angry at the colonial government - landmark event in Australian labor relations
Confederate troops vacate Fort Esperanza, Texas
Battle of Franklin, Tennessee: Confederate attack fails, 7,700 casualties
Battle of Honey Hill, South Carolina (Broad River) 96 dead, 665 wounded
Work begins on 1st US underwater highway tunnel in Chicago
Statue of KingCharles XIIof Sweden dedicated at the King's garden in Stockholm
First international soccer game, Scotland draws with England (0-0) in Glasgow
Opera "El Cid" premieres (Paris)
First commercially successful AC electric power plant opens, Buffalo, NY
The Folies Bergère stages its first revue
1st indoor softball game in Chicago
A German engineer patents front-wheel drive for automobiles
The First Isthmian Canal Commission, appointed by the President having examined possible routes for a canal, issues its report favoring that through Nicaragua over the Panama route
American Old West: Second-in-command of Butch Cassidy's Wild Bunch gang Kid Curry Logan sentenced to 20 years imprisonment with hard labor
Pike Place Market dedicated in Seattle

The US Secretary of State and Japan's ambassador to the US exchange notes in what becomes known as the Root-Takahira Agreement: they affirm support for an independent China with an 'open door' policy and for the status quo in the Pacific
St John Ervine's "John Ferguson" premieres in Dublin
Costa Rica becomes a signatory to the Buenos Aires copyright treaty.
First speed test of the first genuine Japanese aircraft carrier Hōshō
Dutch Catholic minority government of Wilhelm Marx forms
First photo facsimile is transmitted across the Atlantic by radio (London to NYC)
French and Belgium troops withdrawn from their occupation of the Rurh
His Master's Voice and Columbia Records merge into EMI
CCC Camps are established in Cleveland Park District
London's Crystal Palace (built 1851) destroyed by fire
3rd Heisman Trophy Award: Clint Frank, Yale (HB)
Fascist coup in Romania fails
Germany bans Jews being lawyers
Paul Osborn's "Mornings at 7" premieres in NYC
101-year-old Nyack-Tarrytown (NY) ferry makes its last run
-Dec 1st: Sea battle at Tassafaronga, Guadalcanal
German supply vessel Uckermark (formerly called the Altmark) explodes & sinks off Yokohama
U-boats sink and damage 142 allied ships this month (877,774 tons)
Biggest & last British battleship HMS Vanguard launched
Day after UN decree for Israel, Jewish settlements attacked
Baseball's Negro National League disbands
Player-manager Lou Boudreau is selected AL MVP
Soviets set up a separate municipal government in East Berlin
Chinese Communists capture Chongqing
KOTV TV channel 6 in Tulsa, Oklahoma (CBS) begins broadcasting
US President Harry Truman threatens China with atom bomb
Edward Mutesa II, kabaka (king) of Buganda is deposed and exiled to London by Sir Andrew Cohen, Governor of Uganda
French parachutist under Colonel De Castries attacks Dien Bien Phu
20th Heisman Trophy Award: Alan Ameche, Wisconsin (FB)
Ann Hodges is bruised by a meteor at Sylacauga, Alabama, in the first modern instance of a meteorite striking a human
John Strydom succeeds D. F. Malan as premier of South Africa
Argentine government disbands Peronistic party
Thriller novel "The Talented Mr Ripley" by Patricia Highsmith is published in the US
1st use of videotape on TV (Douglas Edwards & the News)
Jon Henricks swims world record 55.4 to win the men's 100m at the Melbourne Olympics; rare Australian 1-2-3 with John Devitt and Gary Chapman taking the minor medals
Milt Campbell sets Olympic record total of 7,937 points to upset fellow American and world record holder Rafer Johnson, and win the decathlon gold medal at the Melbourne Games
1st US guided missile destroyer launched - the Dewey at Bath Iron Works, Maine
WKBW TV channel 7 in Buffalo, NY (ABC) begins broadcasting
Joe Foss named 1st commissioner of AFL
French Senate condemns building own nuclear weapons
Tad Mosels "All the Way Home" premieres in NYC
Billy Williams of the Chicago Cubs is voted NL Rookie of Year
USSR vetoes Kuwaits application for UN membership
Martin Walser's "Überlebensgross Herr Krott" premieres in Stuttgart
USSR launches Zond 2 towards Mars; no data returned
Barbados gains independence from Great Britain (National Day)
Radio time signal WWV moves from Greenbelt, Maryland

British troops leave Aden and the rest of the Federation of South Arabia (FSA) ending British colonial rule; National Liberation Front (NLF) assumes power and renames the state as the People's Republic of South Yemen
Julie Nixon (daughter of former Vice-President Richard Nixon) and David Eisenhower (grandson of former President Dwight D, Eisenhower), announce their engagement
Kuria Muria Islands ceded by Britain to Oman
The Pakistan Peoples Party is founded by Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto who becomes its first Chairman later as the Head of state and Head of government after the 1971 Civil War
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
The government of the Republic of Ireland states that it will take the allegations of brutality against the security forces in Northern Ireland to the European Court of Human Rights
BBC bans Wings' "Hi, Hi, Hi"
Illegal fireworks factory explodes killing 15 in Rome, Italy
Firestone Professional Bowling World Tournament of Champions won by Jim Godman
20th time Islanders shut-out (3-0 vs Canucks)
Dahomey renamed People's Republic of Benin
42nd Heisman Trophy Award: Tony Dorsett, Pittsburgh (RB)
France performs nuclear test
Ted Koppel becomes anchor of late nightly news on Iran "America Held Hostage" (ABC)
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Uruguay's new constitution rejected by referendum

Cold War: In Geneva, representatives from the United States and the Soviet Union begin to negotiate intermediate-range nuclear weapon reductions in Europe (the meetings ended inconclusively on December 17)
South Africa anti-apartheid advocate Bulelani Ngcuka arrested
Ottumwa, Iowa, declared "Video Game Capital of the World" by mayor Jerry Parker
STS-6 vehicle moves to launch pad
US submarine Thomas Edison collides with US Navy destroyer in South China Sea
USSR performs nuclear test
Denver Nuggets coach Doug Moe, hoplessly behind, advises team to let Blazers break their scoring record
Police free kidnapped beer magnate Alfred Heineken in Amsterdam
Radio Shack announces Tandy Model 2000 computer (80186 chip)
Raúl Alfonsín wins Argentine presidential election
Sam Shepards "Fool for love" premieres in NYC
Afghanistan's Constitution adopted
Cyclone lashes Bangladesh, Eastern India; 317 killed
France performs nuclear test at Fangataufa Island
Kohlberg Kravis Roberts & Co. buys RJR Nabisco for $25 billion
Soviets stop jamming Radio Liberty for the first time in 38 years
UN General Assembly (151-2) censures US for refusing PLO's Arafat visa
Deutsche Bank board member Alfred Herrhausen is killed by a Red Army Faction terrorist bomb
FIFA Women's World Cup Final, Tianhe Stadium, Guangzhou, China: Michelle Akers scores twice as US beats Norway, 2-1
Rob Pilatus, 27, of disgraced pop duo Milli Vanilli attempts suicide
San Diego State's Marshall Faulk is 1st freshman to capture national rushing and scoring titles
Intercity-train derailed at Village chief, 5 die
NFL announces 30th franchise - Jacksonville Jaguars
Cruise ship Achille Lauro destroyed by fire at Somalia, 4 die
First Beatles album in 25 years, "Live at the BBC," is released in Britain
Man Mohan Adhikari is sworn in as the first communist Prime Minister of Nepal
Official end of Operation Desert Storm
South African cricket opening batsman Gary Kirsten follows his 1st innings 102 with 133 in the Proteas' 329 run 2nd Test win v India in Kolkata
"Eugene Onegin" closes at Martin Beck Theater NYC
Deutsche Bank announces a US$10 billion deal to buy Bankers Trust, thus creating the largest financial institution in the world
British Aerospace and Marconi Electronic Systems merge to form BAE Systems, Europe's largest defence contractor and fourth largest aerospace firm in the world
In Seattle, Washington, United States, protests against the WTO meeting by anti-globalization protesters catch police unprepared and force the cancellation of opening ceremonies
Department of Homeland Security Secretary Tom Ridge resigns
Lion Air Flight 538 crash lands in Surakarta, Central Java, Indonesia, killing 26
John Sentamu becomes the first black archbishop in the Church of England with his enthronement as the 97th Archbishop of York
South Africa's Civil Union Act of 2006 legalizes same-sex marriage, becomes fifth country in the world and first in Africa to do so
Canada begins its recovery from the recession; economic growth is at 0.4% after 14 months of economic stagnation
At least 32 people are killed in a Ilyushin Il-76 cargo plane crash in the Congo
Australia experiences its hottest spring and second-hottest November recorded
Tabaré Vázquez is re-elected President of Uruguay
21st Conference of the Parties to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change or COP21 begins in Paris
UNESCO adds Belgian beer to its Cultural Heritage List
Def Jam founder Russell Simmons steps down from his companies after allegations of sexual misconduct
Disney announces it has cast Chinese actress Liu Yifei to play Mulan in up-coming live action film
World's longest recorded rainbow lasts for 8 hours and 58 minutes in Taipei's Yangmingshan mountain range
7.0 magnitude earthquake near Anchorage, Alaska
Marriot Hotels reveal massive data breach - 500 million guests affected in one of largest-ever company hacks
Australian cricket batsman David Warner smashes 335 not out in the 2nd Test against Pakistan; second-highest Test score by an Australian (Matt Hayden 380); highest Test score at Adelaide Oval
Gun battle between suspected cartel and security forces at Villa Unión city hall, northern Mexico, kills 21
On the way to 36 in 2nd Test v Pakistan, Australian cricket batsman Steve Smith overtakes Don Bradman’s run-tally of 6,996 Test runs; becomes fastest batsman to 7000, in terms of innings (126)
Alphafold's DeepMind AI program announces it has achieved scientific breakthrough by being able to predict how proteins fold into 3D shapes
Australia condemns doctored photo of Australian soldier threatening Afghan child with knife on Chinese official's Twitter, marking new low in the two countries relationship
Los Angeles County begins three-week stay at home order for 10 million people to combat COVID-19 surge
Newly discovered rock art announced found in the Serranía La Lindosa, Colombian Amazon, dated 12,600 and 11,800 years ago with thousands of paintings of now extinct Ice Age animals
15-year-old student shoots four death and injures seven at Oxford High School in Oxford, Michigan
Artificial intelligence Chatbot ChatGPT prototype released by A.I. lab OpenAI, able to answer complex questions, write poetry and mimic some human emotions
Chinese authorities ease some COVID-19 restrictions in cities including Guangzhou after days of protests
UK Prince and Princess of Wales, William and Katherine arrive in Boston for a US royal visit the same day a former lady-in-waiting resigns in a racism row
UNESCO announces new additions to its Intangible Cultural Heritage list, including the French Baguette, oral traditions of calling camels in the Middle East, and tea practices in China
Russia's Supreme Court labels LGBTQ+ 'movement' an extremist organization, effectively outlawing it
United Nations Climate Change Conference (COP28) opens in Dubai with nations pledging with draft resolution for $400 million fund to help countries most affected by climate change
King of DenmarkCnut the Great[Canute] claims the English throne after the death of Edmund Ironside
First chapter of Order of the Golden Fleece (established byPhilip the Good, in 1430), meets at Saint-Pierre's Collegiate Church in Lille, Duchy of Burgundy
England reconciles with PopeJulius III
Battle at Narva: Swedish forces of KingCharles XIIdefeat Russian army [NS]
Benjamin Franklinreceives the Godfrey Copley medal "on account of his curious Experiments and Observations on Electricity"
PopeLeo XIII's encyclical "Rerum novarum" published

British House of Lords rejectsDavid Lloyd George's 'People's Budget', which tried to shift tax burden to the wealthy. Leads to the Parliament Act; intent to stop unelected house overruling will of the elected house.
Adolf Hitlerspeaks to 50,000 national-socialists in Munich, Germany
Australian cricket legendDon Bradmanmakes an inauspicious Test debut; scores 18 & 1 vs England in 1st Test in Brisbane; dropped to 12th man for 2nd Test
Vladimir K. Zworykinreceives patent on Iconoscope TV system
Carl Gustaf Emil Mannerheimappointed Commander-in-Chief of all Finnish armed services as Soviet forces invade and bomb Helsinki

Bill Terryresigns as supervisor of NY Giants minor league system
New Zealand general election won bySidney Holland's National party, oustingPeter Fraser's Labour government
Jackie Robinsonaccuses the NY Yankees of racial bias on national television

At 21 years, 10 months, 3 weeks, 5 daysFloyd Pattersonbecomes youngest world heavyweight boxing champion; KOsArchie Moorein 5th round in Chicago; first Olympic gold medalist to win a professional heavyweight title
AustralianBetty Cuthberttakes Olympic sprint double when she runs OR equalling 23.4s to win the 200m gold medal at the Melbourne Games; beats Christa Stubnick in repeat of 100m final 4 days earlier
Gert Fredrikssonof Sweden wins his 2nd straight K-1 10,000m canoeing gold medal at the Melbourne Olympics; last time event held in the Summer Olympics; also wins 3rd consecutive K-1 1,000m gold
Assassination attempt on IndonesianPresident Sukarno, kills 8
1960 NFL Draft:Billy Cannonfrom LSU first pick by Los Angeles Rams
U Thantof Burma becomes the 3rd Secretary-General of the United Nations
SenatorEugene McCarthy(D-Minnesota) announces he will run for the US presidency on an anti-Vietnam war platform
A Northern Ireland Civil Rights Association march in Armagh is stopped by Royal Ulster Constabulary because of the presence of a Loyalist counter demonstration led byIan Paisleyand Ronald Bunting
American singer-songwriterNeil Diamondmakes his only appearance on "TheEd SullivanShow", singing "Sweet Caroline" and "Holly, Holy"

Emmy and Peabody Award-winning TV movie "Brian's Song", about the friendship of Chicago Bears football teammates Brian Piccolo andGale Sayers(based on Sayers' autobiography) premieres on ABC, starringJames Caanand Billie Dee Williams
Pink Floyd's album "The Wall" is released, sells 6 million copies in 2 weeks
NY YankeeDave Righettiwins AL Rookie of Year Award
"Gandhi," directed byRichard Attenboroughand starringBen KingsleyandJohn Gielgud, premieres in New Delhi (Best Picture 1983)
"Thriller," the sixth studio album byMichael Jacksonis released (Grammy Award Album of the Year 1984, best-selling album of all time, Billboard Album of the Year 1983)
American-based Czech tennis starIvan Lendlis first player to make over $10 million in career earnings
NYC furrier suesMike Tysonfor $92,000 for non payment of purchase
American actorBurt Lancastersuffers a stroke
US PresidentGeorge H. W. Bushoffers to send Secretary of State James Baker to Baghdad to meet withSaddam Hussein

Ina Garten's cooking TV show "Barefoot Contessa" premieres on the Food Network
Longtime "Jeopardy!" championKen Jenningsof Salt Lake City, Utah finally loses, leaving him with $2,520,700 USD, television's all-time biggest game show haul
RCA releases "Breakaway",Kelly Clarkson's 2nd studio album; wins 2 Grammy Awards and becomes her best selling album to date with over 12 million units sold
The Boston Bruins trade captainJoe Thorntonto the San Jose Sharks
American folk artist "Grandma" Moses' 1943 painting "Sugaring Off" sells for a record $1.3 million for the artist, by Christie's in New York

Hillary Clintonpresidential campaign office hostage crisis: Leeland Eisenberg enters campaign office of Hillary Clinton in Rochester, New Hampshire with suspected bomb and holds three people hostage for 5 hours
José Mujicaannounced as the winner of the run-off election in Uruguay held the day before, and named president-elect
NBA starKobe Bryant(LA Lakers) announces his intention to retire at the end of the season

Harry Stylessingle "As It Was" is the most streamed song globally for Spotify in 2022 withBad Bunnytheir most listened-to artist
